0.title: Reading the Trends Tracker
0.lede: thock's Trends Tracker is a weekly snapshot of what is rising, falling, or holding flat across the hobby — switches, keycaps, layouts, vendors, and brands, scored on a single -100 to 100 scale and updated every Friday. This is what the columns mean and what they do not.

1.title: Pairing Oil Kings with the Mode Sonnet
1.lede: This pairing has been on the bench for months, and a free Sunday finally made it happen. The Sonnet is a soft 65 with a top-mount-leaf gasket, and Oil Kings are a heavy factory-lubed linear — together they hit a register worth coming back to.

2.title: Mode Sonnet R2 opens at CannonKeys
2.lede: Mode has opened the second round of its 65% Sonnet board at CannonKeys. The group buy started 2026-05-01 and runs through 2026-06-15, with new accent-weight color options and global region availability through the same vendor.

3.title: The slow fade of Alice layouts
3.lede: Alice mounts had a clear moment between 2021 and 2023 — the splayed alphas, the angled bar, the promise of a typing posture that didn't twist your wrists. The thock trends snapshot for 2026-W19 pegs Alice movement at -18 and trending down. The layout still has loyal users, but the curve has bent.

4.title: Why the Gateron Oil King sounds the way it does
4.lede: Few stock linears earn the word 'creamy' on first press. The Oil King does, and the reason is not a secret stem material or a proprietary lube — it is a careful pairing of housing resins and a factory tune that other linears under thirty cents tend to skip.

5.title: A beginner's guide to picking your first switch
5.lede: Switches are the single biggest factor in how a keyboard feels and sounds, and the catalog has grown overwhelming. This guide narrows the entire market into three families, explains who each one is for, and gives a three-step path from curiosity to a confident first purchase. By the end, the reader can pick a category without second-guessing.
